description: Low-level memory operations: allocation, typed reads/writes, pointer chains, memory protection, pattern scanning, and heap inspection.

functions.name: alloc
functions.description: Allocates a block of memory of the given size using `malloc` and returns its address.

returns.description: Address of the newly allocated block.

functions.name: free
functions.description: Frees a block of memory previously allocated by `mem.alloc`. Passing an invalid address or double-freeing is undefined behavior.

params.name: address
params.description: Address of the block to free.

functions.name: copy
functions.description: Copies `size` bytes from `src` to `dest` using `memcpy`. The regions must not overlap.

functions.name: fill
functions.description: Fills `size` bytes starting at `address` with the given byte `value` using `memset`.

params.name: value
params.description: Byte value to fill with (only the low 8 bits are used).

functions.name: copy_from_string
functions.description: Copies the raw bytes of a Lua string (including embedded null bytes) to the given address. The number of bytes copied equals the string's length.

params.name: str
params.description: Source data as a (possibly binary) Lua string.

functions.name: read_u8
functions.description: Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer from the given address.

returns.description: Value in the range `[0, 255]`.

functions.name: read_i8
functions.description: Reads a signed 8-bit integer from the given address.

returns.description: Value in the range `[-128, 127]`.

functions.name: read_u16
functions.description: Reads an unsigned 16-bit integer from the given address.

returns.description: Value in the range `[0, 65535]`.

functions.name: read_i16
functions.description: Reads a signed 16-bit integer from the given address.

returns.description: Value in the range `[-32768, 32767]`.

functions.name: read_u32
functions.description: Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer from the given address.

returns.description: Value in the range `[0, 4294967295]`.

functions.name: read_i32
functions.description: Reads a signed 32-bit integer from the given address.

functions.name: read_u64
functions.description: Reads an unsigned 64-bit integer from the given address.
functions.note: Large 64-bit values may lose precision when stored as a Lua `number` (double). Values up to 2^53 are exact.

returns.description: The 64-bit unsigned value as a double.

functions.name: read_i64
functions.description: Reads a signed 64-bit integer from the given address.
functions.note: Large 64-bit values may lose precision when stored as a Lua `number` (double). Values in `[-2^53, 2^53]` are exact.

returns.description: The 64-bit signed value as a double.

functions.name: read_f32
functions.description: Reads a 32-bit IEEE 754 float from the given address and returns it widened to a double.

returns.description: The float value.

functions.name: read_f64
functions.description: Reads a 64-bit IEEE 754 double from the given address.

returns.description: The double value.

functions.name: read_ptr
functions.description: Reads a 64-bit pointer-sized value from the given address.

returns.description: The pointer value as a double.

functions.name: read_string
functions.description: Reads a null-terminated string from memory starting at `address`. If `max_len` is provided, at most that many characters are read (via `strnlen`).

params.name: max_len
params.description: Maximum number of characters to scan for the null terminator.

returns.description: The string read from memory.

functions.name: try_read_u8
functions.description: Protected version of `read_u8`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on access violation or other hardware exception. Does not throw a Lua error.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_i8
functions.description: Protected version of `read_i8`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_u16
functions.description: Protected version of `read_u16`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_i16
functions.description: Protected version of `read_i16`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_u32
functions.description: Protected version of `read_u32`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_i32
functions.description: Protected version of `read_i32`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_u64
functions.description: Protected version of `read_u64`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_i64
functions.description: Protected version of `read_i64`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_f32
functions.description: Protected version of `read_f32`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_f64
functions.description: Protected version of `read_f64`. Returns the value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_ptr
functions.description: Protected version of `read_ptr`. Returns the pointer value on success, or nothing on fault.

returns.description: The pointer value, or nothing if the read faulted.

functions.name: try_read_string
functions.description: Protected version of `read_string`. Uses SEH to catch access violations during the `strnlen` call. If `max_len` is omitted the entire string is read with no length limit. Returns nothing on fault.
functions.note: Unlike the numeric `try_read_*` variants, this uses SEH internally because SSE2-vectorized `strnlen` cannot be safely unwound by the VEH longjmp path.

params.name: max_len
params.description: Maximum number of characters to scan for the null terminator.

returns.description: The string read from memory, or nothing on fault.

functions.name: write_u8
functions.description: Writes an unsigned 8-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_i8
functions.description: Writes a signed 8-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_u16
functions.description: Writes an unsigned 16-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_i16
functions.description: Writes a signed 16-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_u32
functions.description: Writes an unsigned 32-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_i32
functions.description: Writes a signed 32-bit integer to the given address.

functions.name: write_u64
functions.description: Writes an unsigned 64-bit integer to the given address.

params.name: value
params.description: Value to write (converted from double; precision limited to 53 bits).

functions.name: write_i64
functions.description: Writes a signed 64-bit integer to the given address.

params.name: value
params.description: Value to write (converted from double; precision limited to 53 bits).

functions.name: write_f32
functions.description: Writes a 32-bit float to the given address (narrows the double value).

functions.name: write_f64
functions.description: Writes a 64-bit double to the given address.

functions.name: write_ptr
functions.description: Writes a 64-bit pointer-sized value to the given address.

functions.name: write_string
functions.description: Copies a Lua string (plus its null terminator) into memory at the given address. The number of bytes written is `#str + 1`.

functions.name: try_write_u8
functions.description: Protected version of `write_u8`. Returns `true` on success, `false` if an access violation or hardware exception occurred.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_i8
functions.description: Protected version of `write_i8`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_u16
functions.description: Protected version of `write_u16`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_i16
functions.description: Protected version of `write_i16`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_u32
functions.description: Protected version of `write_u32`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_i32
functions.description: Protected version of `write_i32`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_u64
functions.description: Protected version of `write_u64`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_i64
functions.description: Protected version of `write_i64`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_f32
functions.description: Protected version of `write_f32`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_f64
functions.description: Protected version of `write_f64`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_ptr
functions.description: Protected version of `write_ptr`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: try_write_string
functions.description: Protected version of `write_string`. Copies the string and its null terminator into memory. Returns `true` on success, `false` on fault.

returns.description: `true` if the write succeeded, `false` on fault.

functions.name: sizeof
functions.description: Returns the byte size of a named primitive type. Recognized type names are `u8`, `i8`, `u16`, `i16`, `u32`, `i32`, `f32`, `u64`, `i64`, `f64`, and `ptr`. Returns `0` for unrecognized type names.

returns.description: Size in bytes (1, 2, 4, or 8), or `0` for an unrecognized type.

functions.name: unwrap_userdata
functions.description: Extracts the raw pointer address from a light userdata value and returns it as a number.

returns.description: The underlying pointer address.

functions.name: protect
functions.description: Changes the memory protection of a region using `VirtualProtect`. The `rights` string is a combination of the letters `r` (read), `w` (write), and `x` (execute); e.g. `"rwx"`, `"rx"`, `"r"`. Returns `true` on success, `false` on failure.
functions.note: The rights string is parsed character-by-character (case-insensitive). Any combination of `r`, `w`, `x` maps to the corresponding `PAGE_*` constant. An empty or unrecognized string maps to `PAGE_NOACCESS`.

params.name: size
params.description: Size of the region in bytes.

returns.description: `true` if `VirtualProtect` succeeded, `false` otherwise.

functions.name: query
functions.description: Queries virtual memory information for the page containing `address` via `VirtualQuery`. Returns a table of region attributes, or nothing if the query fails.
functions.note: Protection strings use the same format as `mem.protect` plus modifier suffixes: `+g` (guard), `+n` (no-cache), `+w` (write-combine). The `state` field is `"commit"`, `"reserve"`, or `"free"`. The `type` field is `"image"`, `"mapped"`, or `"private"`.

params.name: address
params.description: Any address within the memory region to query.

returns.description: A table with fields: `base` (number), `alloc_base` (number), `size` (number), `state` (string), `protect` (string), `alloc_protect` (string), `type` (string). Returns nothing if `VirtualQuery` fails.

functions.name: deref
functions.description: Follows a multi-level pointer chain starting from `base`. For each offset in the table, the offset is added to the current address and (for all but the last offset) the result is dereferenced as a 64-bit pointer. Returns the final computed address, or nothing if any intermediate pointer is null or causes an access violation.
functions.note: The offsets table is a sequential array. Negative offsets are allowed. After the last offset is applied the result is returned as an address without dereferencing.

params.name: offsets
params.description: Ordered array of integer offsets to apply.

returns.description: The resolved address, or nothing if the chain is broken.

functions.name: heaps
functions.description: Enumerates all committed heap regions in the current process. Returns a table of `{base, size}` entries, one per heap region.

returns.description: Array of tables, each with `base` (number, region start address) and `size` (number, committed bytes).

functions.name: scan
description: Scans a memory region for a byte pattern. The pattern is an IDA-style hex string where each byte is a two-digit hex value and `??` denotes a wildcard byte, e.g. `"48 89 5C ?? ?? 48 8B"`.

When `no_mask` is `false` (the default), the pattern is first parsed for explicit `??` wildcards. If no wildcards are present, an auto-masking heuristic is applied to detect relative offsets and other position-dependent bytes, replacing them with `??` automatically and printing the masked form to the console.
Returns the address of the first match, or nothing if not found.

params.name: base
params.description: Start of the region to scan.

params.name: size
params.description: Length of the region in bytes.

params.name: no_mask
params.description: If `true`, disables auto-masking and treats the pattern as a literal byte string (no wildcard substitution). Defaults to `false`.

returns.description: Address of the first match, or nothing if not found.

functions.name: memory_scan
description: Scans all committed readable pages in the entire process for a byte pattern. Uses the same pattern format and auto-masking logic as `mem.scan`.
Adjacent pages with the same protection flags are coalesced into a single scan region. Regions smaller than `min_size` bytes are skipped.
Returns a table containing the address of every match found.
functions.note: The scan excludes the internal buffer holding the parsed pattern bytes to avoid false positives.

params.name: min_size
params.description: Minimum region size in bytes to include in the scan. Defaults to `0x10000` (65536).

returns.description: Array of addresses (numbers) where the pattern was found. Empty if no matches.

functions.name: addr_to_pat
functions.description: Converts a 64-bit address to an IDA-style little-endian byte pattern string. Useful for building patterns that contain an absolute address.

returns.description: An 8-byte little-endian hex pattern, e.g. `"78 56 34 12 00 00 00 00"`.

functions.name: to_binary_string
functions.description: Copies `size` bytes from `address` and returns them as a binary Lua string. Useful for taking memory snapshots or passing raw data to other APIs.

returns.description: A binary string containing the raw bytes.

functions.name: function_to_ptr
functions.description: Returns the native code pointer of a C function. If the function is not a C function, returns `nil`.

returns.description: The native code address, or `nil` if the function is not a C function.

functions.name: upvalue
functions.description: Retrieves an upvalue from a function by index, bypassing any sandbox restrictions on `debug.getupvalue`. Returns the upvalue's value and its name.

params.name: func
params.description: The function whose upvalue to retrieve.

returns.description: The upvalue's value, or nothing if the index is out of range.
The upvalue's name, or nothing if the index is out of range.
